Paspalum is a type of grass, specifically a genus of plants in the family Poaceae, commonly known as the true grasses. The health benefits of Paspalum are not well documented; however, some species within the genus have been used in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Paspalum include alkaloids, flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, among others. In some traditional medicine systems, Paspalum is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although these are not widely recognized or used in modern medicine.
